authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(116) — Usal Creek and tributaries (Mendocino Co.)
sources.quotedText: Usal Creek and tributaries (Mendocino Co.). Also see Section 8.00(b) Low-Flow Restrictions. Usal Creek main stem below the Usal-Shelter Cove Road. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — hatchery trout/steelhead footnote (adipose fin clip requirement)
sources.quotedText: ** Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: 14 CCR §8.00 — Low-Flow Restrictions (Mendocino Coast streams, subsection (b))
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 236939, "Usal Creek", Stream, CA, Mendocino Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":236939,"gaz_name":"Usal Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Mendocino","objectid_1":1397062} — first point of the multipoint geometry converts to lon -123.85198, lat 39.83238, near Hales Grove. Corrects the file's prior coordinate (-123.833, 39.778), which was off by roughly 6.3 km.

parameters.note: This special regulation applies to the main stem of Usal Creek below the Usal-Shelter Cove Road; tributaries are also covered per the entry heading.

regulations.humanSummary: Special regulation applies to Usal Creek main stem below the Usal-Shelter Cove Road, and tributaries
regulations.verbatimText: Usal Creek and tributaries (Mendocino Co.). Usal Creek main stem below the Usal-Shelter Cove Road

parameters.note: Season spans the calendar-year boundary: open from the fourth Saturday in May through the following March 31; closed from April 1 through the day before the fourth Saturday in May.

regulations.humanSummary: Open fourth Saturday in May through March 31
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

parameters.note: Applies from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31.

parameters.note: Applies from November 1 through March 31; bait and lures are allowed but only barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used from November 1 through March 31
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead per day, combined; 4 in possession, combined.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag, 4 in possession
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Only trout/steelhead showing a healed adipose fin clip may be retained as "hatchery" fish; wild fish with the adipose fin intact must be released immediately and do not count toward the bag or possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: Wild (adipose-fin-intact) steelhead and trout must be released unharmed; only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery fish may be kept
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Usal Creek is also subject to Section 8.00(b) Low-Flow Restrictions, which can close fishing when streamflow drops below a specified threshold; consult 14 CCR §8.00(b) and the CDFW Low-Flow Fishing Regulations page for current status.

regulations.humanSummary: Also subject to Section 8.00(b) low-flow closure restrictions

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
